project.name: Personal Website Frontend
project.description: Vue 3 + Vite + TypeScript personal portfolio SPA with typed content modules, section-composed homepage, and GitHub Pages deployment

metadata.role: Static personal website showcasing career stories, projects, and resume

memories.title: Typed content-driven portfolio SPA
memories.content:
Typed content-driven portfolio SPA

Role in project: Personal portfolio SPA where homepage sections and project case studies are driven by typed content modules and composed section components

Advantages over alternatives: Editorial updates stay in TypeScript content files with compile-time validation instead of a CMS or markdown pipeline for a single-author site

Adoption rationale: Personal portfolio SPA where homepage sections and project case studies are driven by typed content modules and composed section components. Advantages over alternatives: Editorial updates stay in TypeScript content files with compile-time validation instead of a CMS or markdown pipeline for a single-author site.

Used Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite as Personal portfolio SPA where homepage sections and project case studies are driven by typed content modules and composed section components, leveraging Editorial updates stay in TypeScript content files with compile-time validation instead of a CMS or markdown pipeline for a single-author site.

memories.adoption_rationale: Personal portfolio SPA where homepage sections and project case studies are driven by typed content modules and composed section components. Advantages over alternatives: Editorial updates stay in TypeScript content files with compile-time validation instead of a CMS or markdown pipeline for a single-author site.

memories.title: GitHub Pages static deployment
memories.content:
GitHub Pages static deployment

Role in project: Automated build-and-deploy pipeline producing static dist output to GitHub Pages with custom domain CNAME

Advantages over alternatives: Zero server maintenance; push-to-master triggers type-checked production build and Pages deploy

Adoption rationale: Automated build-and-deploy pipeline producing static dist output to GitHub Pages with custom domain CNAME. Advantages over alternatives: Zero server maintenance; push-to-master triggers type-checked production build and Pages deploy.

Used GitHub Actions, Vite as Automated build-and-deploy pipeline producing static dist output to GitHub Pages with custom domain CNAME, leveraging Zero server maintenance; push-to-master triggers type-checked production build and Pages deploy.

memories.resume_statement: Used GitHub Actions, Vite as Automated build-and-deploy pipeline producing static dist output to GitHub Pages with custom domain CNAME, leveraging Zero server maintenance; push-to-master triggers type-checked production build and Pages deploy.

memories.title: Vue 3
memories.content:
Vue 3: Renders the SPA with Composition API script-setup SFCs; HomePage composes section components fed by typed content props

Role in project: Renders the SPA with Composition API script-setup SFCs; HomePage composes section components fed by typed content props

Typical purpose: Progressive JavaScript framework for building reactive user interfaces

Advantages over alternatives: Lightweight runtime for a content-heavy static site; script setup keeps section components concise without a global state library

Adoption rationale: Renders the SPA with Composition API script-setup SFCs; HomePage composes section components fed by typed content props. Advantages over alternatives: Lightweight runtime for a content-heavy static site; script setup keeps section components concise without a global state library.

memories.title: Vite
memories.content:
Vite: Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules

Role in project: Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules

Typical purpose: Fast frontend build tool with native ESM dev server and Rollup-based production builds

Advantages over alternatives: Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output

Adoption rationale: Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules. Advantages over alternatives: Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output.

Used Vite as Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules, leveraging Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output.

memories.typical_purpose: Fast frontend build tool with native ESM dev server and Rollup-based production builds
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output
memories.adoption_rationale: Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules. Advantages over alternatives: Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output.
memories.resume_statement: Used Vite as Dev server, production bundler, and @ alias resolver mapping @ to src for imports across pages and content modules, leveraging Near-instant HMR for iterative content and layout work; vue-tsc + vite build script enforces type-check before static output.

memories.title: TypeScript
memories.content:
TypeScript: Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract

Role in project: Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract

Typical purpose: Static typing for JavaScript applications to catch schema drift at compile time

Advantages over alternatives: Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge

Adoption rationale: Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract. Advantages over alternatives: Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge.

Used TypeScript as Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract, leveraging Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge.

memories.typical_purpose: Static typing for JavaScript applications to catch schema drift at compile time
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge
memories.adoption_rationale: Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract. Advantages over alternatives: Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge.
memories.resume_statement: Used TypeScript as Defines HomePageContent, ProjectEntry, and related interfaces so homepage and project pages share a typed content contract, leveraging Content modules import types from a single contract file; vue-tsc blocks builds when section props or content shapes diverge.

memories.title: Vue Router
memories.content:
Vue Router: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior

Role in project: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior

Typical purpose: Official client-side router for Vue.js single-page applications

Advantages over alternatives: Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output

Adoption rationale: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior. Advantages over alternatives: Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output.

Used Vue Router as SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior, leveraging Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output.
memories.role_in_project: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior
memories.typical_purpose: Official client-side router for Vue.js single-page applications
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output
memories.adoption_rationale: SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior. Advantages over alternatives: Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output.
memories.resume_statement: Used Vue Router as SPA routing for home, project detail (slug param), about, resume, and contact with lazy-loaded page components and scroll-to-top behavior, leveraging Code-split page bundles via dynamic import(); createWebHistory suits GitHub Pages hosting behind a static dist output.

memories.title: CSS custom properties
memories.content:
CSS custom properties: Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles

Role in project: Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles

Typical purpose: Design tokens as CSS variables for consistent theming without a CSS preprocessor

Advantages over alternatives: No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site

Adoption rationale: Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles. Advantages over alternatives: No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site.

Used CSS custom properties as Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles, leveraging No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site.

memories.typical_purpose: Design tokens as CSS variables for consistent theming without a CSS preprocessor
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site
memories.adoption_rationale: Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles. Advantages over alternatives: No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site.
memories.resume_statement: Used CSS custom properties as Centralizes dark-theme palette, typography scale, spacing, and accent colors consumed by layout and section component styles, leveraging No Tailwind or Sass dependency; layered @import chain (tokens → base → patterns → layout → section-components) keeps styling maintainable for a small site.

memories.title: GitHub Actions
memories.content:
GitHub Actions: CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com

Role in project: CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com

Typical purpose: Hosted CI/CD for building and deploying repositories

Advantages over alternatives: Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME

Adoption rationale: CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com. Advantages over alternatives: Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME.

Used GitHub Actions as CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com, leveraging Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME.

memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME
memories.adoption_rationale: CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com. Advantages over alternatives: Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME.
memories.resume_statement: Used GitHub Actions as CI pipeline on master push: npm ci, vue-tsc + vite build, upload dist artifact, deploy to GitHub Pages with custom domain therealdom.com, leveraging Free static hosting integrated with the repo; no separate CDN or server config beyond Pages permissions and CNAME.

memories.title: Content-as-data modules
memories.content:
Content-as-data modules: Homepage, projects, and about copy live in src/content/*.ts as typed exports rather than hard-coded in templates

Role in project: Separates editorial content from presentation so copy updates do not require touching Vue markup

Advantages over alternatives: TypeScript interfaces enforce section shape; easier to diff content changes than scattered template strings

Adoption rationale: Separates editorial content from presentation so copy updates do not require touching Vue markup. Advantages over alternatives: TypeScript interfaces enforce section shape; easier to diff content changes than scattered template strings.
memories.role_in_project: Separates editorial content from presentation so copy updates do not require touching Vue markup
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: TypeScript interfaces enforce section shape; easier to diff content changes than scattered template strings
memories.adoption_rationale: Separates editorial content from presentation so copy updates do not require touching Vue markup. Advantages over alternatives: TypeScript interfaces enforce section shape; easier to diff content changes than scattered template strings.

memories.title: Section composition
memories.content:
Section composition: HomePage imports discrete section SFCs (HeroSection, TimelineSection, etc.) and passes slices of homeContent as props

Role in project: Maps each homepage anchor (stories, career, stack, about) to an isolated component with a narrow prop contract

Advantages over alternatives: Each section can evolve independently; jump-nav hrefs align with section IDs without a monolithic homepage template

Adoption rationale: Maps each homepage anchor (stories, career, stack, about) to an isolated component with a narrow prop contract. Advantages over alternatives: Each section can evolve independently; jump-nav hrefs align with section IDs without a monolithic homepage template.
memories.role_in_project: Maps each homepage anchor (stories, career, stack, about) to an isolated component with a narrow prop contract
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Each section can evolve independently; jump-nav hrefs align with section IDs without a monolithic homepage template
memories.adoption_rationale: Maps each homepage anchor (stories, career, stack, about) to an isolated component with a narrow prop contract. Advantages over alternatives: Each section can evolve independently; jump-nav hrefs align with section IDs without a monolithic homepage template.

memories.title: Lazy route loading
memories.content:
Lazy route loading: Router routes use () => import('@/pages/...') for all page components

Role in project: Splits project detail and secondary pages into separate chunks loaded on navigation

Advantages over alternatives: Smaller initial bundle for the homepage-first visitor path; standard Vue Router pattern without extra lazy-load library

Adoption rationale: Splits project detail and secondary pages into separate chunks loaded on navigation. Advantages over alternatives: Smaller initial bundle for the homepage-first visitor path; standard Vue Router pattern without extra lazy-load library.
memories.role_in_project: Splits project detail and secondary pages into separate chunks loaded on navigation
memories.advantages_over_alternatives: Smaller initial bundle for the homepage-first visitor path; standard Vue Router pattern without extra lazy-load library
memories.adoption_rationale: Splits project detail and secondary pages into separate chunks loaded on navigation. Advantages over alternatives: Smaller initial bundle for the homepage-first visitor path; standard Vue Router pattern without extra lazy-load library.

memories.title: Layout shell
memories.content:
Layout shell: App.vue renders PageShell which owns site chrome (header nav, mobile menu) and RouterView for page content

Role in project: Keeps navigation and accessibility scaffolding in one layout while pages focus on section content

Advantages over alternatives: Consistent nav and mobile toggle behavior across routes without duplicating header markup per page

Adoption rationale: Keeps navigation and accessibility scaffolding in one layout while pages focus on section content. Advantages over alternatives: Consistent nav and mobile toggle behavior across routes without duplicating header markup per page.

memories.title: Static SPA: Vite builds to dist/; GitHub Pages serves index.html and assets with...
memories.content: Static SPA: Vite builds to dist/; GitHub Pages serves index.html and assets with createWebHistory routing (no SSR).

memories.title: Data flow: src/content/*.ts exports → page/section components via typed props → ...
memories.content: Data flow: src/content/*.ts exports → page/section components via typed props → scoped or global CSS from tokens.css layer chain.

memories.title: Project detail pages resolve slugs via getProjectBySlug in projects.ts and rende...
memories.content: Project detail pages resolve slugs via getProjectBySlug in projects.ts and render fullStory blocks split by paragraph.

memories.content: Resume PDF served from public/DamanveerSingh_Resume.pdf; portable Node under .tools/ optional for Windows environments without system npm.
